questions.q: Chittorgarh is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh is a great hill fort in present-day Rajasthan.

questions.q: Chittorgarh was long the capital of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh served for centuries as the capital of Mewar.

questions.q: The rulers who held Chittorgarh as their seat belonged to which Rajput clan?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh was the seat of the Sisodia rulers of Mewar.

questions.q: Chittorgarh is best described as which kind of site?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh is a great hill fort, set atop a plateau in Rajasthan.

questions.q: Chittorgarh is famous as the site of which dramatic acts during sieges?
questions.explanation: The fort is renowned for famous sieges and acts of jauhar.

questions.q: Which legendary queen is most closely associated with Chittorgarh?
questions.explanation: The legendary Rani Padmini is most famously linked to Chittorgarh.

questions.q: Chittorgarh is celebrated as a symbol of which group's resistance and honour?
questions.explanation: The fort stands as one of the most storied symbols of Rajput history and honour.

questions.q: The shorter traditional name often used for Chittorgarh fort is which of these?
questions.explanation: The fort and its town are commonly called simply Chittor.

questions.q: Jauhar at Chittorgarh refers to which act?
options: A victory parade after a siege
Self-immolation by women to avoid capture
questions.explanation: Jauhar was the collective self-immolation of women to avoid capture during sieges.

questions.q: Chittorgarh fort is set atop what kind of landform?
questions.explanation: The fort sits on an elevated rocky plateau rising above the surrounding plain.

questions.q: Which Delhi Sultanate ruler besieged Chittor around 1303 CE?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji besieged and captured Chittor around 1303 CE.

questions.q: Which Sisodia ruler recaptured and restored Mewar's power at Chittor in the 15th century, building the Vijaya Stambha?
questions.explanation: Rana Kumbha strengthened Chittor and built the Vijaya Stambha (Tower of Victory).

questions.q: The Vijaya Stambha at Chittorgarh is famous as what kind of monument?
questions.explanation: The Vijaya Stambha is a celebrated tower of victory built by Rana Kumbha.

questions.q: Which Gujarat sultan sacked Chittor in 1535, prompting a famous jauhar?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah of Gujarat besieged Chittor in 1535, leading to a major jauhar.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor captured Chittor in 1568, after which Mewar's capital shifted away from it?
questions.explanation: Akbar captured Chittor in 1568; Mewar's later capital moved to Udaipur.

questions.q: After Chittor fell to the Mughals, which city became the new Mewar capital?
questions.explanation: Mewar's rulers founded and shifted their capital to Udaipur after losing Chittor.

questions.q: Tradition holds that Chittor faced how many major jauhars in its history?
questions.explanation: Tradition records three great jauhars at Chittor (1303, 1535, and 1568).

questions.q: The Kirti Stambha (Tower of Fame) at Chittorgarh is associated with which religion?
questions.explanation: The Kirti Stambha is a Jain tower of fame within the Chittorgarh fort.

questions.q: Chittorgarh fort was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site as part of which 2013 grouping?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh is one of the Hill Forts of Rajasthan listed by UNESCO in 2013.

questions.q: The 16th-century mystic poet-saint Mirabai is traditionally associated with Chittor through marriage into which house?
options: The Sisodia royal house of Mewar
questions.explanation: Mirabai is traditionally tied to the Sisodia royal house of Mewar at Chittor.

questions.q: The poem Padmavat, which dramatizes Chittor's siege, was composed by whom?
questions.explanation: Malik Muhammad Jayasi composed the Padmavat about the legend of Padmini at Chittor.

questions.q: Which Rana, defeated by Babur at Khanwa in 1527, ruled Mewar from Chittor?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga (Sangram Singh) ruled Mewar from Chittor and fought Babur at Khanwa.

questions.q: Which Rajput chief is said to have heroically led the defence during Akbar's 1567 to 1568 siege of Chittor?
questions.explanation: Jaimal and Patta are celebrated for leading Chittor's defence against Akbar in 1567 to 1568.

questions.q: Which Sisodia ruler is traditionally credited with first wresting Chittor for the Sisodia line in the 14th century after the Khalji conquest?
questions.explanation: Rana Hammir Singh is traditionally credited with re-establishing Sisodia rule over Chittor.

questions.q: The legend of Padmini links Alauddin Khalji's 1303 siege of Chittor to which Rajput king of Mewar?
questions.explanation: The Padmini legend names Rawal Ratan Singh as the Mewar king during the 1303 siege.

questions.q: The Vijaya Stambha built by Rana Kumbha commemorated a victory over which adversary?
questions.explanation: The Tower of Victory is traditionally said to mark Rana Kumbha's triumph over the Malwa Sultanate.

questions.q: How many gates (pols) traditionally guard the approach up to Chittorgarh fort?
questions.explanation: Seven massive gates (pols) traditionally guard the winding ascent to the fort.

questions.q: The figure of Padmini is regarded by historians as which of the following?
options: A largely legendary figure first popularized by later poetry
questions.explanation: Padmini is widely treated as a legendary figure popularized by Jayasi's 16th-century Padmavat, not firm history.

questions.q: Which large water bodies were a notable feature of Chittorgarh fort's self-sufficiency in sieges?
questions.explanation: The fort contained many reservoirs and ponds that helped it withstand long sieges.

questions.q: Rana Udai Singh II, who relocated Mewar's capital to Udaipur, was the father of which famous Mewar ruler?
questions.explanation: Udai Singh II, who founded Udaipur after Chittor's fall, was the father of Maharana Pratap.
